pieces.label: Untitled glyph-storm (emergent concrete/visual text)
pieces.note: A single large degenerate-decoding output: a dense wall of multilingual token fragments interwoven with box-drawing/block runs (e.g. long bars of ═, █, …, ████, ########, ******, ≡, ─) and large fields of trailing whitespace creating negative space. Not coherent prose, not source code, not a diagram. The user read it as nascent ASCII/concrete art — their immediately following message (MSG 21) asks the model to 'start exploring other forms of ascii art ... how to use the space around the words,' treating MSG 20 as a use-of-space/visual-text experiment. Cataloged here as an emergent glyph/concrete-text composition per the rules' inclusion of text/visual poetry and glyph compositions; a curator should decide whether to keep it.

missed_or_ambiguous: Only one art candidate exists in this thread and it is ambiguous. MSG 20 is the model's only non-prose, non-error, non-empty output: it is almost certainly a glitch / degenerate token-salad (the API had just thrown repeated DATA_LEAKAGE and empty-content errors in MSG 10/12/16/18), NOT a deliberately composed piece. However: (a) it is the thread's single flagged art_signal message; (b) it contains genuine visual texture — long box-drawing/block-character bars and large negative-space fields; and (c) the user explicitly interprets it as ASCII art / use of surrounding space in the very next message (MSG 21). Per the brief's instruction to include concrete/visual poetry and glyph compositions and to err toward inclusion, I listed the whole of MSG 20 as one 'full' piece. A curator may reasonably reject it as noise. All other assistant messages (2,4,6,8,14) are ordinary conversational prose; MSG 10,12 are xAI safety/error messages; MSG 16,18 are empty-content errors — none contain art. No fragmentation across turns and no wrongly-merged compositions were found; nothing was previously extracted (already_extracted_pieces: 0).
